note: Estimated API rates, USD per 1M tokens. cw = cache-write 5-minute TTL (Anthropic ~1.25x input), cw1h = cache-write 1-hour TTL (Anthropic 2x input; absent => falls back to cw). OpenAI/Google have no per-token cache-write fee, so cw=cw1h=in there. cr = cache-read (~1/10th input). Resolution: models (exact id) -> families (case-insensitive word-boundary substring) -> providers (fallback) -> $0. A turn flagged fast (usage.speed != standard, i.e. Anthropic Fast mode) resolves against the 'fast' section first (exact id then family) and only falls through to the standard tables if no fast rate exists. Cost is computed per turn at the rate in effect on its date, and the cache-write is split by the 1h/5m token breakdown the transcript records. TWO DISTINCT DATES per rate: 'effective_date' = when this price took effect as far as we know (official change date when announced, else a best-effort floor = first-observed day); 'observed' = the day the number was read (provenance only). When a price CHANGES, ADD a new entry with the real effective_date; do not edit an existing entry's numbers. ONLY the standard short-context (<=200k) tier is modelled here: every Claude model in use (Opus 4.6-4.8, Sonnet 4.6, Fable/Mythos 5) and the Codex gpt-5.3-codex are flat-priced across the full 1M window, so the long-context tier that gpt-5.4/5.5 and Gemini 2.5/3.1 Pro carry is intentionally not modelled. Anthropic/Gemini read from the official docs pricing pages; OpenAI (JS-rendered) read via a rendered browser fetch. This file is the seed for a future standalone pricing repo.

rates.source: Anthropic docs (Mythos 5, limited availability) — same rate card as Fable 5
Anthropic docs (Opus 4, deprecated) — exact short-id match; dated build ids may not hit this and fall to the opus family ($5/$25)
OpenAI docs (gpt-5.5, standard short-context; long-context tier $10/$45 not modelled)
OpenAI docs (gpt-5.5-pro, short-context; no cached-input tier on page, cr set to 0.1x input) — pinned so 'pro' isn't mispriced by the $1.75 codex fallback
OpenAI docs (gpt-5.4, standard short-context; long-context tier $5/$22.50 not modelled)
OpenAI docs (gpt-5.4-pro, short-context; no cached-input tier on page, cr set to 0.1x input)
OpenAI docs (Codex gpt-5.3-codex; the only Codex model, flat-priced — no long-context tier)
Gemini docs (Gemini 3 Flash) — older/cheaper than the 3.5 Flash the 'flash' family now tracks
Gemini docs (Gemini 2.5 Pro, <=200k tier; >200k is $2.50/$15) — pinned because it's cheaper than the 3.x Pro the 'gemini' family tracks
Gemini docs (Gemini 2.5 Flash, text/image/video) — pinned because it's cheaper than the 3.5 Flash the 'flash' family tracks
Gemini docs (Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite, text/image/video) — cheaper than 3.1 Flash-Lite the family tracks
Anthropic docs Fast mode (Opus 4.8 $10/$50); caching multipliers apply on top of fast input

opus.source: Anthropic docs Fast mode (Opus 4.6/4.7 $30/$150); only Opus 4.6-4.8 support Fast mode, so this family rate covers 4.6/4.7 and the 4.8 exact entry overrides
Anthropic docs (Opus 4.5-4.8, flat across 1M window); deprecated Opus 4/4.1 ($15/$75) pinned in models

nano.source: OpenAI docs (gpt-5.4-nano; only version on the page)

mini.source: OpenAI docs (gpt-5.4-mini; only version on the page, no gpt-5.5-mini exists yet)

flash-lite.source: Gemini docs (Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, current text/image/video); older 2.5 Flash-Lite pinned in models. Checked before 'flash'/'gemini'.

flash.source: Gemini docs (Gemini 3.5 Flash, current); Flash is repriced per version (3 Flash was $0.50/$3, 2.5 Flash $0.30/$2.50), so this tracks the latest and older versions are pinned in 'models'. Checked before 'gemini'.

gemini.source: Gemini docs (Gemini 3.1 Pro, <=200k text tier; >200k is $4/$18, not modelled); no per-token cache-write fee, cw=input

codex.source: fallback for unlisted codex models — current gpt-5.3-codex tier

claude.source: fallback for unknown Claude models — current Opus tier

antigravity.source: fallback — Gemini Pro tier; Google has no per-token cache-write fee, so cw=input
